name: review-depth-api-design
author: diffscope
description: API design, contract validation, and breaking-change benchmark pack for deeper live review runs.

fixtures.name: ts-breaking-api-field-rename

expected_findings.description: Renaming a response field from user_id to userId is a backwards-incompatible API contract change for existing clients.
contains_any: version or deprecate the old field

fixtures.description: Public API responses should not silently rename fields without a compatibility strategy.

fixtures.name: python-missing-input-validation

expected_findings.description: Endpoint no longer validates that order_id is positive, so negative identifiers are accepted by the public API.
contains_any: public endpoint accepts invalid negative values

fixtures.description: Public endpoints should preserve basic input validation and reject invalid identifier ranges.

fixtures.name: go-removed-required-field

expected_findings.description: Removing a required protobuf field is a breaking schema change for downstream consumers that still send or expect region.
contains_any: region field was part of the contract

fixtures.description: Schema definitions should not remove required fields from published contracts without a compatibility plan.

fixtures.name: rust-public-api-error-type-change

expected_findings.description: Changing a public function from anyhow::Result to Result<_, PublishError> is a breaking API signature change for library callers.
contains_any: requires a semver-major change or compatibility shim

fixtures.description: Public Rust APIs should treat return-type changes as contract breaks and gate them behind compatibility planning.
